description: A person steps through the doorway and exits the house. Looking at the same threshold from inside, 'thiru' is the crossing outward from a place, state, or activity. 'mue' (OUT.OF) names that interior, while 'pholeni' (depart) can leave any source without asserting an inside edge.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip lightly against or just beyond the upper front teeth and pass unvoiced air through the gap for 'th', then move the tongue forward for stressed high-front unrounded 'i'. Make a tap or short trill with the tongue tip at the alveolar ridge and finish with high-back rounded 'u'.
usage_notes: The interior commonly follows 'mue' (OUT.OF). With causative 'ka', an agent causes another person or thing to cross outward.

semantic_domains.activity: crossing outward from a bounded place or activity
